channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How President Trump is reshaping transgender people’s rights
item.description: Coming up at 9 a.m. on Tuesday, MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests break down President Donald Trump’s series of executive orders that significantly reshape transgender rights in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Nikhil Kumaran, MPR News

item.title: NAACP guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The advisory also lists companies that have recommitted to DEI in recent weeks – such as Delta Airlines, Apple and Ben & Jerry’s.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE's access to critical IRS system containing taxpayer information is imminent
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency, led by Elon Musk, will soon have access to an IRS system that contains sensitive taxpayer information, Fox News has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio meets Saudi Arabian officials ahead of US-Russia talks expected to lay groundwork for Trump-Putin summit
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman and Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan al-Saud in Ridyah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tearful chair of Munich Security Conference expresses 'fear' in farewell address after blistering Vance speech
item.description: The outgoing chairman of the Munich Security Conference delivered an emotional speech that ended in tears after taking a swipe at Vice President JD Vance's blistering speech.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk says millions in Social Security database are between ages of 100 and 159
item.description: Elon Musk indicated that a Social Security database lists millions of centenarians "with the death field set to FALSE.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: A conservative activist who echoed Donald Trump’s baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol rioters has been nominated by the Republican president to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington, D.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A fired national park ranger lost his dream job. He says the public is losing more
item.description: Brian Gibbs was an environmental educator at Effigy Mounds National Monument in Iowa. His job was among those nixed by the Trump administration as it pushes to shrink federal spending.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman, MPR News

item.title: GOP lawmakers divided on Musk, seen by some as a liability
item.description: Republicans on Capitol Hill are divided over tech billionaire Elon Musk being the public face of President Trump’s aggressive bid to shrink the government, with some souring on Musk’s prominent role as he increasingly has become a target of Democratic attacks.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump admin aims for killing blow to independence of 'Deep State' agencies
item.description: Trump's DOJ sent Sen. Durbin a letter Wednesday notifying him that the department plans to ask SCOTUS to overturn a key precedent case limiting the president's power to remove independent agency members.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin sending foreign minister to Saudi Arabia for talks with Trump officials
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will meet with his Russian counterpart in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday to discuss a possible peace deal for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Duffy brings up Clinton while noting SpaceX workers will visit Air Traffic Control System Command Center
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy mentions Hillary Clinton while noting that SpaceX workers will visit the Air Traffic Control System Command Center
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump names Jim Byron to senior archivist role
item.description: “Jim will manage the National Archives on a day-to-day basis, while we continue our search for a full-time Archivist,” Trump posted.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic governors diverge on how to handle Trump
item.description: Democratic governors are diverging in their approaches to President Trump amid mounting speculation that some of them are gunning for the White House in 2028. Some are digging their heels into resistance efforts, as in Illinois, where Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes laps on Daytona 500 speedway in The Beast
item.description: “This is your favorite president,” Trump said. “I’m a big fan. I’m a really big fan of you people. How you do this, I don’t know. But I just want you to be safe.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Europe tries to muscle up as U.S. plots mediation to help end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: European leaders are taking steps to answer assertive U.S. efforts to end Russia’s war against Ukraine. France on Sunday announced a meeting of continental leaders and Baltic countries called for new steps to strengthen Kyiv’s hand in any future talks.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump says 14th Amendment is for former slaves, not migrants
item.description: On Thursday, a fourth federal judge blocked the executive order issued by the president on his first day in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats' headaches multiply in battle for Senate
item.description: Correction: The dates of Sens. Warner's and Shaheen's last wins have been corrected. Minnesota Sen. Tina Smith’s (D) decision to not run for reelection in 2026 is adding another headache for Democrats trying to win back control of the Senate in two years.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: George has his day, and so does Abe. But states honor US presidents in lots of ways
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Is Presidents Day the most confusing holiday in the U.S.? States seem to have as many names for it and ideas about whom to honor as there have been presidents. The federal government doesn't even recognize Monday as Presidents Day.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Area 51: Fact and fiction
item.description: The Groom Lake, Nevada, facility — about 100 miles northwest of Las Vegas — remains highly secretive.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats nervously wait on Trump response to court rulings
item.description: House Democrats howling over the flood of unilateral actions streaming from the White House are voicing confidence that the courts will provide a check on potential abuses of power. They’re less certain, however, about how President Trump will respond.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department fires 20 immigration judges from backlogged courts amid major government cuts
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The Trump administration fired 20 immigration judges without explanation, a union official said Saturday amid sweeping moves to shrink the size of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The latest: Federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: Federal workers all over the country have responded with anger and confusion Friday toward President Donald Trump’s aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump moves with dizzying speed on his to-do list. But there are warning signs in his first month
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As President Donald Trump approaches the first-month mark in his second term, he has moved with dizzying speed and blunt force to reorder American social and political norms and the economy while redefining the U.S. role in the world.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, JOSH BOAK and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungary's transformation into an 'electoral autocracy' has parallels to Trump's second term
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hours before President Donald Trump was sworn in to begin his second term, promising a “golden age” for America, the leader of a Central European country was describing the years ahead in strikingly similar terms.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How changes to a CDC vaccine panel under Kennedy could reshape policy
item.description: A committee of experts that advises the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention is critical in setting national vaccine policy. It's also vulnerable to political interference.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: House Republicans plot impeachment against judges blocking Trump, DOGE
item.description: Some House Republicans are pledging to bring up impeachment articles against federal judges blocking Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats see small wins in low-key races, fueling hopes for November
item.description: Democrats are already seeing some small victories in under-the-radar races in the beginning of 2025, with the party hoping they can maintain that energy heading into November as they look to flip the Virginia governor’s mansion and hold off GOP opponents in other elections.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump officials signal potential changes at NOAA, the weather and climate agency
item.description: Federal workers at the home agency for the National Weather Service are concerned about a potential overhaul by Trump officials focused on cutting government costs.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda, Michael Copley, and Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Trump's framed mug shot hangs outside Oval Office
item.description: A framed image of President Donald Trump's mug shot from when he was indicted in Georgia in 2023 is hanging outside the Oval Office in the latest bit of redecorating since he moved back in.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Alleged DOGE employees show up at San Francisco City Hall
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- An official with the San Francisco Sheriff’s Office confirmed to NewsNation affiliate KRON4 that it is investigating a report of three individuals claiming to be Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) employees who attempted to enter offices in San Francisco City Hall on
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department moves to dismiss Eric Adams case
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally asked a federal district court to drop its corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) after federal prosecutors in Manhattan resigned rather than dismiss the case.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: US Army bans transgender people from enlisting
item.description: The new policies, effective immediately, could affect more than 10,000 active duty troops, per AP estimates.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: University of St. Thomas scrambling after Dept. of Ed cuts millions in funding
item.description: The $6.3 million grant was targeted under President Donald Trump’s effort to cut programs the government considers “diversity, equity and inclusion,” or DEI.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gretchen Brown, and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. confirmed as Trump's health secretary after close Senate vote
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as President Donald Trump’s health secretary, putting the prominent vaccine skeptic in control of $1.7 trillion in federal spending, vaccine recommendations and food safety as well as health insurance programs for roughly half the country.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
